summary: Iteration 4 testing - Layout bug fixes verification. Celestial Objects and Timeline sections look correct. Critical bug found in SolarSystem3D.js: onWheel missing e.preventDefault() and undefined onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave handlers in cleanup.

ui_bugs.issue: onWheel handler missing e.preventDefault() and {passive: false} option - scroll wheel inside canvas still scrolls the page
onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave are referenced in cleanup (lines 253-254) but never defined - will throw ReferenceError on component unmount

action_items: Fix SolarSystem3D.js onWheel: add e.preventDefault() and register with {passive: false}: renderer.domElement.addEventListener('wheel', onWheel, {passive: false})
Fix SolarSystem3D.js: define onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave handlers that lock/unlock body overflow, or remove the cleanup references to undefined variables (lines 253-254)
critical_code_review_comments: SolarSystem3D.js line 194-197: const onWheel = (e) => { ... } - missing e.preventDefault(). Event added without {passive: false} so browser won't allow preventDefault anyway
SolarSystem3D.js lines 253-254: References onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave in cleanup but these variables are never defined in the component - will throw ReferenceError
context_for_next_testing_agent: APIs all working: /api/objects (16), /api/timeline (25), /api/space/past (20). Celestial Objects section: auto-fit grid with 16 cards visible, Saturn rings visible, object-fit contain working, text below image. Timeline: 360px wide cards visible. SolarSystem3D has 2 bugs to fix: (1) onWheel missing preventDefault+passive:false, (2) onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave referenced in cleanup but undefined. Planet buttons may need scrolling deeply to reach.
rca_of_issue: SolarSystem3D.js onWheel was implemented without e.preventDefault() call and without the {passive: false} EventListenerOptions flag. Modern browsers default to passive:true for wheel events to improve scroll performance, which disallows calling preventDefault(). Fix: add e.preventDefault() inside handler AND add {passive: false} as third argument to addEventListener. Additionally, the cleanup function references onMouseEnter and onMouseLeave event handler variables that were never declared - likely intended for body overflow locking feature that was partially implemented.
